title: Housing and Development Bank (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Housing and Development Bank in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Housing and Development Bank?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank (HDB) is an Egyptian commercial bank established to address the country's housing shortage and urban development needs. Originally founded as a state-owned enterprise, HDB has evolved into a fully-fledged universal bank offering comprehensive retail, corporate, and investment banking services. Listed on the Egyptian Stock Exchange under the ticker HDBK, the bank maintains a dual focus on its founding mission of affordable housing finance while serving as a major player in Egypt's broader banking sector. As of 2024, HDB operates approximately 100 branches and more than 440 ATMs across all Egyptian governorates.

faq.question: When was Housing and Development Bank founded?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank was founded on June 30, 1979 in Cairo, Egypt. The bank was established by the Egyptian government to alleviate critical housing shortages driven by rapid population growth and urbanization. HDB's original mandate was to provide long-term financing for housing projects and infrastructure development, making homeownership accessible to low- and middle-income families. The bank was created as part of Egypt's national housing policy to support urban planning and residential development across the country. Since 1995, HDB has been traded on the Egyptian Stock Exchange, transitioning from a fully state-owned enterprise to a publicly traded company while maintaining significant government and institutional shareholdings.

faq.question: Where is Housing and Development Bank headquartered?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank is headquartered at 26 El Koroum Street, Mohandessin, Giza, Egypt. The headquarters in Giza, part of the Greater Cairo metropolitan area, serves as the bank's central administrative and operational hub. From this location, HDB manages its nationwide network and coordinates its banking operations across Egypt's diverse urban and developing regions.

faq.question: Where does Housing and Development Bank operate?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank operates throughout Egypt with a nationwide presence spanning all governorates. The bank has strategically expanded its branch network to serve both established urban centers and rapidly developing areas. HDB maintains significant operations in Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, and the new administrative capital. The bank also serves emerging urban developments including New Cairo, 6th of October City, and Sheikh Zayed. With approximately 100 branches and over 440 ATMs distributed across Egypt, HDB ensures accessibility for customers in major cities, provincial capitals, and developing communities aligned with the country's urban expansion initiatives.

faq.question: What services does Housing and Development Bank offer?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank provides a comprehensive range of banking and financial services across multiple business segments: Housing and Real Estate Finance: Mortgage products, real estate loans, housing project financing, and participation in government social housing programs including the Social Housing Project and National Housing Program. Retail Banking: Current accounts, savings accounts, deposit accounts, personal loans, credit cards, debit cards, prepaid cards, and consumer finance products. Corporate Banking: Business accounts, corporate loans and facilities, working capital finance, trade finance, financial derivatives, and treasury services for medium and large enterprises. SME Banking: Specialized financing solutions for small and medium-sized enterprises, including microfinance through subsidiary El Tameer for Microfinance. Investment Services: Asset management, financial advisory, company merger and restructuring services, portfolio management through subsidiary Obelisk Portfolio Management, securities trading through HD Trading Securities, and securitization services. Digital Banking: HDB Mobile Banking app available on iOS and Android, HDB NET online banking platform, fund transfers (internal, external, and international), bill payments through Fawry integration, mobile account opening, digital loan applications, and soft token authentication for secure transactions.

faq.question: What makes Housing and Development Bank unique?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank is distinguished by its specialized focus on housing finance and urban development, a mission that has defined the institution since its founding in 1979. Unlike traditional commercial banks, HDB combines comprehensive banking services with deep expertise in real estate and infrastructure financing. Government Partnership: HDB serves as a key implementing partner for Egypt's national housing initiatives, directly supporting government programs to provide affordable housing for low- and middle-income citizens and contributing to the development of new cities and communities. Real Estate Ecosystem: The bank has built an extensive network of subsidiaries covering the entire real estate value chain, including development companies, property management firms, mortgage finance companies, and real estate investment funds, creating an integrated approach to housing solutions. Market Leadership: HDB ranks as the fourth-largest mortgage provider under government subsidy programs in Egypt, having disbursed EGP 7.92 billion to over 76,000 clients and capturing 9.4% of the total financing for low-income housing as of 2024. Strong Financial Performance: The bank reported a 90% surge in net profits to $244 million in 2024, demonstrating robust growth and operational efficiency in a competitive banking environment. Digital Transformation: HDB has invested significantly in modernizing its technology infrastructure, offering comprehensive digital banking services through mobile and online platforms that enable customers to perform complex transactions including account opening and loan applications entirely online.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Housing and Development Bank?
faq.answer: Housing and Development Bank is classified as a universal bank (or full-service commercial bank) in the Egyptian banking system. While it began as a specialized housing finance institution, HDB has successfully transformed into a comprehensive commercial bank offering the full spectrum of banking services. HDB operates as a joint-stock company (S.A.E. - Société Anonyme Égyptienne) publicly traded on the Egyptian Stock Exchange. The bank is regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt and operates under Egyptian banking law. The bank's ownership structure includes significant stakes from Egyptian state entities (New Urban Communities Authority holds 29.81%, Misr Life Insurance 9.09%, Misr Insurance 8.29%, Social Housing and Mortgage Finance Fund 7.41%, and Egyptian Endowment Authority 5.03%), alongside major private investors including Saudi Arabian businessmen through Dubai-based companies (RELACO 9.99% and REMCO Egypt 9.84%), with approximately 20.53% in free float. Despite its evolution into a universal bank, HDB maintains its distinctive identity as Egypt's premier housing and development finance specialist, differentiating it from purely commercial banking institutions.
